answers.question: Single worker thread or small pool?
answers.answer: Small pool (2-4 threads). Allows parallel list comprehensions within a single test. Nested comprehensions like `[[j for j in range(3)] for i in range(3)]` need concurrent generators.

answers.question: When to create the worker thread(s)?
answers.answer: Lazy initialization. Create pool on first mb_generator_create() call. No overhead for tests that don't use generators.

updated_requirements: Replace per-generator thread::spawn in generator.rs with a lazy-initialized pool of 2-4 reusable worker threads. Workers receive (body_fn, args, channels, shared_capture) tuples via a shared job channel and execute generator bodies. Workers set up thread_local GEN_TX/GEN_RX per job. Pool is created on first generator creation. This avoids cumulative pthread lifecycle issues that cause EXC_BAD_ACCESS code=257 on macOS aarch64 after ~130 generator thread spawns.
